threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still expect the Tube to run on time. Locals know it's a game of patience and improvisation here.
Last night on the N68, a man carried a cardboard cutout of Duran Duran. He told everyone he was their 'new bassist' and started singing 'Hungry Like the Wolf.'

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: You know you’re in Neukölln when the guy at the corner bakery knows your order and the local park turns into an open-air gallery every weekend.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery—graffiti-covered walls and all, serving the best döner in town.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name the bus route by the number of people waiting at 16th and Mission, even in the rain.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading passengers on a crowded 27 bus today. He had an old guitar and a killer voice—made the delays feel like a concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the locals love the noisy terraces, but it's the late-night trash collection that keeps us up. You notice it after a while.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their oversized map, looking lost at the local markets, while the rest of us know shortcuts through the alleys.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘The Dolphin’ is just a pub name, not a ‘community centre’ for everyone’s dogs.
Heard a guy on the N35 last night bragging about how he dodged fare evasion cameras by wearing a hat. Thought it was a strange flex for 3am.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a pink tutu effortlessly cycling past a group of bemused tourists near Tempelhofer Feld.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar that serves beer brewed from recycled water. It’s a conversation starter, if nothing else!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's from the Mission when they know the exact time the next bus is late because they've timed their coffee runs to match. No spreadsheets, just instinct.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo hop on the 19, holding a bouquet. The bus driver didn’t even blink. Just another Friday night in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the Gaudí hype, but the real charm is in the small plaça bars where locals sip vermut and swap gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them still looking at maps on their phones, confused at the tram stops, while the locals just hop on and off like it’s second nature.
